items.title: Ex-FIFA prez backs call to 'stay away' from WC
items.summary: Former FIFA president Sepp Blatter on Monday backed a proposed fan boycott of World Cup matches in the United States because of the conduct of President Donald Trump and his administration at home and abroad.

items.title: Source: USMNT's Freeman set for Villarreal move
items.summary: LaLiga side Villarreal is finalizing the acquisition of United States and Orlando City defender Alex Freeman, a source with knowledge of the deal told ESPN.

items.title: PSG complete signing of Barça teen Fernández
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain have completed the signing of Barcelona's talented teenager Dro Fernández after exercising his €8 million ($9.5 million) release clause for the LaLiga side.

items.title: Stanford, family of Katie Meyer reach settlement
items.summary: Stanford announced a settlement with the family of Katie Meyer, the former star soccer goalkeeper and two-time team captain who died by suicide in 2022.

items.title: Who has made Troy's Premier League team of the week?
items.summary: After every round of Premier League matches this season, Troy Deeney gives us his team of the week. Do you agree with his choices?

items.title: AZ bereid om mee te werken aan verhuur van gewilde verdediger
items.summary: AZ staat open voor een verhuur van Billy van Duijl, die in de concrete belangstelling van onder meer PEC Zwolle staat. De verdediger moet er nog even goed over nadenken.

items.title: Van Duiven gaat van PSV naar Spanje: 'Heracles kan altijd nog'
items.summary: Robin van Duiven maakt het seizoen nog af bij Jong PSV en stapt vervolgens transfervrij over naar Real Valladolid. Dat terwijl Heracles Almelo ook interesse had in de jonge spits. Van Duiven springt echter bewust in het diepe, geeft hij aan.

items.title: Barcelona left angry after Dro Fernández rejects contract and agrees €8m PSG move
items.summary: 18-year-old was expected to stay with Hansi Flick’s side Joan Laporta describes move as ‘an unpleasant situation’ Barcelona have confirmed the departure of talented young midfielder Dro Fernández to Paris Saint-Germain with the club president, Joan Laporta, describing the situation around his move as “unpleasant”. Dro, who joined the club at 14, made his first-team debut in September, and turned 18 on 12 January, arrived in the French capital on Sunday night for a medical. Considered one of the outstanding players of his generation, his representatives had appeared to agree a new contract with Barcelona, due to be signed after his birthday, but he informed the head coach, Hansi Flick, of his decision to depart the same week. Continue reading...

items.title: Barry goal rescues draw for Everton against Leeds
items.summary: Watch Premier League highlights as Everton's Thierno Barry continues his good run of form with a second-half goal to earn the Toffees a 1-1 draw with Leeds United.

items.title: Koploper ADO kan opeens niet meer winnen: 'Paniek heeft geen enkele zin'
items.summary: ADO Den Haag maakte maandag een einde aan de reeks verliespartijen, maar na het doelpuntloze gelijkspel tegen Vitesse 'was het nog steeds geen feest', stelde aanvoerder Jari Vlak. De koploper van de Keuken Kampioen Divisie blijft wachten op zijn eerste zege van 2026.

items.title: Farioli ziet onfeilbaar Porto koppositie in Portugal verder verstevigen
items.summary: FC Porto heeft goede zaken gedaan in de strijd om de landstitel. De Portugese topclub rekende op eigen veld af met Gil Vicente. Daardoor is de koppositie verstevigd.

items.title: Girona-debuut Ter Stegen gaat niet onopgemerkt voorbij
items.summary: Marc-André ter Stegen kan met een goed gevoel terugkijken op zijn debuut voor Girona. Diep in blessuretijd had hij een cruciale redding in huis, waarmee de keeper een punt redde voor zijn ploeg in het thuisduel met Getafe.

items.title: Barry scores again to earn Everton draw against Leeds
items.summary: Thierno Barry continued his strong scoring form with a second-half equaliser to earn Everton a 1-1 home draw against Leeds.

items.title: Thierno Barry makes profligate Leeds pay to earn Everton a point
items.summary: Thierno Barry’s debut season in English football must have tested the patience of David Moyes at times but the Everton manager’s perseverance is being rewarded. The £27m summer signing from Villarreal struck his fourth goal in five Premier League games as Everton turned the tide on Leeds. A point apiece reflected a dominant half apiece, though both managers felt they could have had more. Continue reading...

items.title: Bayern Munich open talks to extend Kane contract
items.summary: Bayern Munich sporting director Max Eberl says the German club are in talks with Harry Kane about extending the striker's contract.

items.title: Russell 'impressed' as Red Bull surprise themselves at first F1 test
items.summary: Isack Hadjar admitted his surprise at Red Bull's "productive" and "smooth" performance after topping the timesheet at the first day of Formula 1 pre-season testing in Barcelona.

items.title: Carragher: Slot's job at risk if Liverpool miss out on Champions League
items.summary: Jamie Carragher believes Arne Slot's job will be at risk if Liverpool fail to qualify for the Champions League this season.

items.title: Carrick’s cult of common sense gets Manchester United believing again
items.summary: There is no special trick to the interim manager’s early success, just a commitment to sound and sensible thinking What must Ruben Amorim make of it? Maybe that 3-4-2-1 might not be the answer for this Manchester United team? Perish the thought. The club’s recently sacked manager was clear that not even the pope would make him change – presumably because Leo XIV is also a big fan of three centre-halves. Saying that, Amorim did come close to losing his religion towards the bitter end, however brief and unconvincing his dalliance with a back four was. He reverted to a three for his final game at Leeds in early January. As the dust settles on Michael Carrick’s second thrilling win as the United interim manager in two matches, the last-gasp triumph at Arsenal following the home win against Manchester City, it is a moment, first and foremost, for the club’s supporters to savour. Continue reading...

items.title: Nottingham Forest make £35m bid for Crystal Palace striker Jean-Philippe Mateta
items.summary: French forward has told Palace he wants to leave Forest also interested in Wolves’ Strand Larsen Nottingham Forest have made a £35m bid to sign Jean-Philippe Mateta from Crystal Palace. The France striker, who has told Palace he wants to leave, is among Forest’s targets as they look to strengthen in attack. Forest signed the 6ft 7in forward Lorenzo Lucca on loan from Napoli last week with an option to make the deal permanent. Only Wolves (15) have scored fewer top-flight goals this season than Forest (23). Continue reading...

items.title: Celtic to appeal Trusty red at Hearts
items.summary: Celtic will appeal the red card shown to Auston Trusty during Sunday's 2-2 draw at Hearts.
